title: Define and implement an interface
instruction:
An interface declares methods without implementation. Classes implement them.

Run this.
successMessage: Both Circle and Square implement IShape. The foreach treats them identically through the interface.

title: Multiple interfaces
instruction:
A class can implement multiple interfaces.

Run this.
successMessage: Duck implements both interfaces. It can be used as either IFlyable or ISwimmable depending on context.

title: Interface as parameter type
instruction:
Accepting an interface type makes functions work with any implementation.

Run this.
successMessage: PrintDocument accepts any IPrintable. Adding a new document type only requires implementing IPrintable — no changes to PrintDocument.

title: Default interface methods
instruction:
C# 8+ allows default method implementations in interfaces.

Run this.
successMessage: ConsoleLogger only implements Log(). LogInfo and LogError use the default implementations that call Log(). Existing implementations are not broken when you add default methods.

title: IComparable<T>
instruction:
Implementing `IComparable<T>` allows sorting with built-in methods.

Run this.
successMessage: IComparable<T> defines how to compare two instances. List.Sort() uses CompareTo to sort by Grade.

title: IEnumerable<T>
instruction:
Implement `IEnumerable<T>` to make your type usable in foreach.

Run this.
successMessage: yield return produces values lazily. NumberRange is now foreach-able and LINQ-compatible — all from implementing IEnumerable<T>.

title: Interface segregation
instruction:
Many small interfaces beat one large interface.

Run this.
successMessage: Separate interfaces allow passing the minimal contract. A function needing only IReadable does not force the caller to provide write/seek capability.

title: Explicit interface implementation
instruction:
Explicitly implement when two interfaces have the same method name.

Run this.
successMessage: Explicit implementation resolves name conflicts. The method is only callable through the interface reference, not through the concrete type directly.
